Off Calendar Jan-04-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julia Lingys for and with Mary Condy. Lawrence Siracusa for Andrea Leung.

Mr. Siracusa presented argument stating that the monies were paid directly to Mary Condy, that it changed when his client came in, and that there aren't enough funds to pay the claim, that they are short approxiamately $36,000.00, that Mary Mayo is aware of it, that all of the extraordinary fees have been paid, that no statutory fees have been paid pending this Court's decision, and that they are happy to get Kerry Duff's waiver. Ms. Lingys agreed with Mr. Siracusa's comments noting that the money did come directly to Mary Condy. The matter was taken under submission. Mr. Siracusa stated that he wanted to recalculate the interest and then email the order to the Examiner. The Court stated that it would not review and sign the order until January 4, 2011. Ms. Lingys confirmed the reserve of $44,000.00.

The Court stated that there was no issue with the request to reduce bond. Mr. Siracusa presented oral argument to support his position with regard to the request to deny the petition to determine entitlement. The Court stated that the Petition to Determine Entitlement was not on calendar today, that there was a notice issue, suggested filing a separate petition for instructions setting forth all of the assets as of the date of death and making it clear as to when the merge took place, and that it was unclear as to the source of funds. Mr. Siracusa will file a declaration asking the Court to withdraw the request for a ruling on the Petition For an Order Denying Petition to Determine Entitlement.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Thirkell for Mary Condy. Roberta Economidis for Mary Condy. Zack Georgopoulus for Mary Condy. Lawrence Siracusa for Andrea Leung. James Topinka for The Whisk Group LLC.

Mr. Thirkell stated that there is an urgency, that the assignment will close today, and that after the Court ruled at the last hearing that a lot happened out in the hallway. Mr. Siracusa stated that he did not oppose the order to shorten time but he did oppose the stay request. Arguments regarding the stay were held. MINI MINUTES: Lori Lutzker appearing for Joseph Condy. Edward A. Koplowitz appearing for Aqua Corporation. Gregory O'Keeffe appearing for Christopher Condy. Mary Mayo appearing for Kerry Condy Duff. Kenneth Horowitz appearing for petitioner Mary Condy. Edward Thirkell appearing for Mary Condy as beneficiary. Zach Georgopoulos retained as counsel for the transition. Eric Starr appearing for Mina Group. Court's tentative ruling is to deny the petition. Ownership and control of the assets are in question. Mr. Horowitz stated that the estate has 100% interest of the assets and he addressed the need of selling it. Mr. Koplowitz raised the threshold issue that the petition and reply are not verified. Mr. Thirkell questioned who Mr. Koplowitz represented. Mr. O'Keeffe stated he represents Christopher Condy. The order appointing administrator included the filing of an inventory and appraisal before sale is requested. Mr. Horowitz stated that the Probate Referee requested for financial statements for the last couple years. He is unable to get them. Ms. Lutzker did not concede to the administrator has the authority to sell. Ms. Mayo reported that the creditor's claims is about $2,000,000.00. The children are the creditors and they have filed their claims. Mr. Starr expressed his client's interest to buy the restaurant. Petition is denied. Court is not making any instruction to Mrs. Condy. Ms. Lutzker will prepare the order.
